user_query:
Please use the provided generator to create a nested archive set, then recursively extract everything and produce a precise file report.

Steps:
1) From the workspace root, run: python3 input/generate_archives.py. This will create input/archives/ containing:
   - top.zip with these contents at its top level:
     - readme.txt (text file)
     - inner.tar.gz which, when extracted, contains nested/data.csv and nested/more/notes.txt.gz (a single-file gzip of notes.txt)
     - extra.tgz which contains alpha.txt
     - another.zip which contains doc.md and pack.tar (pack.tar contains leaf.txt)
   - solo.txt.gz (a single-file gzip of solo.txt)
   The text contents are fixed and deterministic.

2) Recursively extract all archives matching input/archives/* into output/extracted (use a glob so the destination is honored). Ensure the extraction places .extracted_success marker files in each directory created for an extracted archive.

3) Create output/report.json as a JSON array of exactly these 7 final plain text files (no others), with objects having fields: path (relative to output/extracted), sha256 (hex lowercase), and size (bytes):
   - top/readme.txt
   - top/inner/nested/data.csv
   - top/inner/nested/more/notes.txt/notes.txt
   - top/extra/alpha.txt
   - top/another/doc.md
   - top/another/pack/leaf.txt
   - solo.txt/solo.txt

4) The extracted directory structure must reflect archive stems (e.g., notes.txt.gz becomes notes.txt/notes.txt). Do not include archives or marker files in the report; only the 7 plain text files above.

Write all extracted artifacts under output/extracted and the report to output/report.json.
